title: Bind every new Grabowski checkout to owner, TTL and closure
goal: Extend the existing safe checkout lifecycle so newly created operator worktrees require owner, purpose, source task or PR, retention and artifact class, and so terminal clean checkouts become archive-and-cleanup candidates after a grace period.

approval.note: Source changes and read-only verification are authorized. Runtime deletion or cleanup effects require a fresh exact-target plan and post-readback.

acceptance.assertion: Worktree creation fails closed when owner, purpose, retention and source binding are absent.
Per-repository active and completed-retained checkout limits are enforced without deleting existing foreign checkouts.
Task or PR completion can produce a recovery archive and cleanup dry-run candidate after a 24-hour grace period.
Dirty state, active tasks, processes, resource leases and main worktrees remain hard blockers.

metadata.source: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1 plan from live 2026-07-14 storage audit
does_not_establish: permission to bypass leases or dirty-state guards
metadata.readiness_basis: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1-T002 verified by merged Heim-PC PR #47, green CI, byte-identical merge readback, live managed-build receipt and installed operator projection.
